# QUELO 2.3.1 Released!
### April 22, 2026
### QUELO 2.3.1 features vast performance improvement, user interface refinement, and centralized cloud resource tracking among other enhancements.
## Performance: QUELO-AI 4x Speedup
![](https://qsimulate.com/images/product/quelo-2-3-1-fig1.png)
Combining algorithmic enhancements, refinement of simulation parameters, and optimization of the simulation engine, QUELO 2.3.1 achieved up to a **4x speedup** for QUELO-AI simulation compared to the 2.3.0 release. This results in reduced computational expenses and a faster time to solution. Together with advanced runtime scheduling capability, this sets QUELO apart from its competitor.
## Core FEP Workflow Enhancement
![](https://qsimulate.com/images/product/quelo-2-3-1-fig2.png)
The core FEP workflow has been significantly enhanced in QUELO 2.3.1. First, a new panel has been added to allow users to **inspect the alignment** of molecules prior to starting an FEP simulation. Second, users can now modify or merge the perturbation map and post-process data using **Network Hysteresis Correction (NHC) analysis**. Furthermore, QUELO now provides an option to **extend active or completed simulations** , enabling users to increase simulation time in case more extensive configurational sampling is necessary.
## User Experience Improvement
![](https://qsimulate.com/images/product/quelo-2-3-1-fig3.png)
QUELO 2.3.1 features a new UI designed to dramatically improve the user experience compared to 2.3.0 version. The panel for **selecting quantum mechanical (QM) regions** has been overhauled to streamline the process. This release is also equipped with **cloud resource tracking utilities** that visualize current and historical usage levels of the platform. We would like to thank our customers, as many of the new features have been developed in response to their feedback!
![logo dark](https://qsimulate.com/images/nav-bar/logo-dark.svg)
## QUELO
### Next-Generation Quantum Mechanics Revolutionizes Drug Lead Optimization.

QUELO 2.3.1 Released!

April 22, 2026

QUELO 2.3.1 features vast performance improvement, user interface refinement, and centralized cloud resource tracking among other enhancements.

Performance: QUELO-AI 4x Speedup

Combining algorithmic enhancements, refinement of simulation parameters, and optimization of the simulation engine, QUELO 2.3.1 achieved up to a 4x speedup for QUELO-AI simulation compared to the 2.3.0 release. This results in reduced computational expenses and a faster time to solution. Together with advanced runtime scheduling capability, this sets QUELO apart from its competitor.

Core FEP Workflow Enhancement

The core FEP workflow has been significantly enhanced in QUELO 2.3.1. First, a new panel has been added to allow users to inspect the alignment of molecules prior to starting an FEP simulation. Second, users can now modify or merge the perturbation map and post-process data using Network Hysteresis Correction (NHC) analysis. Furthermore, QUELO now provides an option to extend active or completed simulations, enabling users to increase simulation time in case more extensive configurational sampling is necessary.

User Experience Improvement

QUELO 2.3.1 features a new UI designed to dramatically improve the user experience compared to 2.3.0 version. The panel for selecting quantum mechanical (QM) regions has been overhauled to streamline the process. This release is also equipped with cloud resource tracking utilities that visualize current and historical usage levels of the platform. We would like to thank our customers, as many of the new features have been developed in response to their feedback!

QUELO

Next-Generation Quantum Mechanics Revolutionizes Drug Lead Optimization.
